title: CLI Invoked Identity
goal: Implement #608 by exposing a compact invoked-CLI identity payload in normal JSON outputs so host repos can distinguish executable provenance from managed payload health.
non_goals: Do not implement repo-owned compatibility expectations or drift enforcement from #609/#610 in this slice.
Do not require a specific package manager or make unknown/global invocation a hard failure.

intent.outcome: config/status/doctor and startup/report surfaces can include the identity of the currently invoked CLI.
intent.constraints: Keep identity provider-neutral and separate from payload drift; avoid shell-specific process forensics.
intent.latitude: Choose conservative fields available from Python runtime/package metadata.
intent.escalation: Escalate if implementation requires broad config semantics or compatibility enforcement.

execution.next_step: Find existing config/status/report payload assembly and add a reusable CLI identity payload.
execution.proof: Targeted tests plus proof-selected commands for changed paths.

invariants: workspace.cli_invoke remains command guidance, not proof of current invocation.
managed payload health remains separate from invoked executable identity.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Host repos can detect CLI version/source drift after updates.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After invoked CLI identity is visible in compact outputs.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Future slices can compare invoked identity against repo expectations and report executable drift.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Compatibility config and drift warning enforcement.

intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm output is useful for host repo drift reasoning without overclaiming compatibility enforcement.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add a conservative invoked-CLI identity payload.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not make compatibility enforcement claims.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Field names and exact placement in compact outputs.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A package-manager-specific dependency check seems required.

adaptive_assurance.reason: This affects startup/status trust for host repos but is not high-risk domain code.

common_risks: coupling identity to one package manager
mixing executable identity with payload health

active_milestone.scope: Expose invoked CLI identity in compact outputs.

immediate_next_action: Locate config/status/report/start payload assembly and add a reusable CLI identity payload.
invariants: Identity output is descriptive and does not enforce compatibility.
Payload drift status remains a separate diagnostic.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Use a reusable invoked_cli_identity payload with conservative confidence fields.
open_questions_to_close: Which compact surfaces should carry the identity payload in this first slice?

completion_criteria: Config JSON reports invoked CLI identity.
Status/doctor or common lifecycle output includes the same identity without conflating it with payload health.

execution_run.what happened: Added invoked_cli_identity payload assembly to config, report, lifecycle status/doctor, start, and startup-report outputs. Kept lifecycle/startup identity compact and full diagnostic identity available through config and report.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI payload assembly, CLI tests, report/startup schemas, planning state and execplan
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py
execution_run.next step: Continue parent lane with #609 repo-owned CLI compatibility expectations.

system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can a host repo tell what CLI executable produced the answer?

durable_residue.learned constraint: Cross-output runtime identity should have one reusable schema owner instead of duplicated schema definitions.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Schema duplication increases drift risk when a payload appears in both startup and report contracts.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: When compatibility expectation schemas are added or identity appears in another output schema.
